hypothesis: Baseline: group=32 tile=64 beta=0.267 ndsp=32 pte=256
rtl_note: Reference point. All subsequent experiments compared here.
paper_sentence: H1-baseline: FOM +0.0%. Achieves 2.09x compression, 2.09x BW speedup, norm_err=0.2402, 3278 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: Reduce group size to 16: less WHT mixing -> lower error, more per-element overhead
rtl_note: RTL: 4-stage butterfly instead of 5. Scale UINT16 unchanged. 1.06 b/elem overhead (vs 0.53).
paper_sentence: H2-group16: degrades FOM by -11.2%. Compression 1.95x, BW speedup 1.95x, norm_err=0.3067. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Increase group size to 64: more WHT mixing -> better compression, larger errors
rtl_note: RTL: 6-stage butterfly, INT64 intermediates wider range. 0.27 b/elem overhead.
paper_sentence: H3-group64: FOM +7.7%. Achieves 2.15x compression, 2.15x BW speedup, norm_err=0.1875, 3278 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: Group size 128: maximum compression ratio, expect error degradation
rtl_note: RTL: 7-stage butterfly. WHT intermediate width = 16+7=23 bits (still fits INT32). 0.13 b/elem overhead.
paper_sentence: H4-group128: FOM +6.0%. Achieves 2.20x compression, 2.20x BW speedup, norm_err=0.1444, 3278 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: Higher beta* divisor -> harder to be Q4 -> more Q8 -> better quality, less compression
rtl_note: CSR: TIU_BETA_STAR register value increases. More Q8 tokens in MHC.
paper_sentence: H5-beta-conservative: degrades FOM by -18.9%. Compression 1.93x, BW speedup 1.93x, norm_err=0.2407. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Lower beta* divisor -> easier to be Q4 -> more Q4 -> more compression, higher error
rtl_note: CSR: TIU_BETA_STAR register value decreases. More Q4 tokens in MHC.
paper_sentence: H6-beta-aggressive: FOM +1.9%. Achieves 2.42x compression, 2.42x BW speedup, norm_err=0.2399, 3278 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: LACU tile size 128: fewer softmax rescales (seq/128 vs seq/64), larger on-chip footprint
rtl_note: RTL: ping-pong buffer doubles. Fewer exp() operations per decode step. Latency may improve.
paper_sentence: H7-tile128: degrades FOM by -13.8%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: LACU tile size 32: more softmax rescales, potentially better numerical precision
rtl_note: RTL: buffer halves. More frequent rescale overhead. Tradeoff for non-standard seq_len.
paper_sentence: H8-tile32: degrades FOM by -14.5%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Double DSP count to 64: 2x compute throughput, check if still compute-bound
rtl_note: ZCU102: 64/2520=2.5% DSP utilisation. Ridge point moves to 1.5 FLOP/B DRAM. Still compute-bound.
paper_sentence: H9-ndsp64: FOM +34.1%. Achieves 2.09x compression, 2.09x BW speedup, norm_err=0.2402, 5112 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: 128 DSPs: 4x throughput, roofline may shift to memory-bound for typical workloads
rtl_note: ZCU102: 128/2520=5.1% DSP. Ridge point 3.0 FLOP/B. Actual OI=2.0 -> memory-bound! DSP headroom wasted.
paper_sentence: H10-ndsp128: FOM +38.8%. Achieves 2.09x compression, 2.09x BW speedup, norm_err=0.2402, 7097 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.

hypothesis: Expand PTE to 1024 entries: 4x more tokens can live in SRAM
rtl_note: RTL: PTE SRAM = 1024 x 12-bit addr x 3 fields. Needs wider seq_pos field (10-bit vs 8-bit).
paper_sentence: H11-pte1024: degrades FOM by -53.8%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Uniform quantization instead of Lloyd-Max: simpler hardware (no LUT), expect higher error
rtl_note: RTL: eliminates 256-entry Q4/Q8 codebook LUT. Saves ~512 B SRAM. Tradeoff vs accuracy.
paper_sentence: H12-uniform-codebook: degrades FOM by -45.5%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.0520. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Aggressive eviction (retain 30%): more tokens evicted, SRAM holds fewer but most important
rtl_note: TIU: lower threshold -> more EVICT tags -> MHC cold tier fills faster.
paper_sentence: H13-retain30: degrades FOM by -53.8%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Conservative eviction (retain 70%): keep most tokens, SRAM fills faster
rtl_note: TIU: higher threshold -> fewer evictions -> SRAM pressure increases.
paper_sentence: H14-retain70: degrades FOM by -53.8%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

hypothesis: Double attention sinks to 8: more tokens bypass eviction scoring
rtl_note: TIU: first 8 tokens always RETAIN. Helps models with distributed sink patterns.
paper_sentence: H15-sink8: degrades FOM by -53.8%. Compression 2.09x, BW speedup 2.09x, norm_err=0.2402. Design parameter discarded: tradeoff not Pareto-improving.

rtl_note: Combined RTL implications from all kept experiments.
paper_sentence: H_composed: FOM +189.5%. Achieves 2.57x compression, 2.57x BW speedup, norm_err=0.1442, 7097 decode steps/s @ 300 MHz.
